mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound compresses the equity risk premium discount rate, sustaining a persistent tailwind for equity valuations. As long as FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, forward S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon should be positively biased relative to historical unconditional average.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09 percent, near zero lower bound
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during ZIRP periods are not statistically greater than the full-sample unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05), or rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate declines by at least 0.2 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window from an elevated post-recession level, it signals accelerating labor market healing that supports consumer income and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by >= 0.2pp over trailing 3 months while UNRATE level remains above 6.5%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.6 percent, elevated post-recession level
failure_condition: Triggered instances show 20-day forward returns not significantly different from non-triggered baseline (p > 0.05), or hit rate for positive direction falls below 55% over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates broad-based consumer and investment demand strength that is not captured by either series alone. When both series post positive month-over-month growth simultaneously, forward S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days are positively biased due to reinforced earnings growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth > 0 and HOUST MoM growth > 0 concurrently
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 422794 million dollars, HOUST at 914 thousand units
regime_hint: early-to-mid cycle expansion with recovering housing demand
failure_condition: Joint-trigger periods do not outperform single-series trigger periods by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.10), or rank IC of the interaction term is not incrementally positive versus either univariate proxy over a 2-year rolling test.

mechanism_narrative: During an economic recovery regime following a downturn, sustained month-over-month gains in industrial production (INDPRO) reflect reaccelerating manufacturing capacity utilization, which forward-predicts rising corporate profits and positive equity returns over a monthly horizon. Outside recovery regimes this relationship is expected to be muted or absent.
failure_condition: INDPRO trend signal fails to produce rank IC above 0.03 during recovery-labeled periods over any 3-year rolling window, or returns are not distinguishable from expansion-regime baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in CPIAUCSL above a 0.4% threshold signals an inflation surprise that raises market expectations of future Federal Reserve tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
regime_hint: low nominal rate regime with inflation surprise risk
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following triggered CPI surprises are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) or the effect reverses sign in more than 40% of triggered instances over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent positive trend in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) reflects ongoing labor market expansion that supports aggregate income and consumption, providing a steady positive drift to S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon regardless of the specific macro regime.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year rank IC of PAYEMS trend versus 60-day forward returns falls below 0.02, or positive payroll trend periods fail to outperform negative trend periods at p > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-interest-rate regime where cheap financing should stimulate housing investment, a stagnant or declining housing starts (HOUST) series signals unresolved structural demand weakness that offsets monetary accommodation, predicting negative or muted S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon. This relationship is regime-specific and should reverse once rates normalize.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average declines while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 914 thousand units, FEDFUNDS at 0.09 percent
regime_hint: zero interest rate policy with weak housing recovery
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in triggered periods are not significantly negative versus unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or the effect disappears once tested across multiple ZIRP episodes.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of near-zero federal funds rate and elevated unemployment rate above 7% represents maximum policy slack, under which markets price in an extended period of monetary accommodation. This joint state, distinct from either variable alone, produces a short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 returns as investors price in low discount rates without near-term tightening risk.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09 percent and UNRATE at 7.6 percent concurrently
regime_hint: extended monetary accommodation amid labor market slack
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during joint-trigger windows show hit rate below 52% or are statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (p > 0.10) over a 3-year walk-forward evaluation.
